title: plectranthus chiangdaoensis
intro: Plectranthus chiangdaoensis, also known as Thai balm, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to Thailand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Thai balm include the relief of headaches, fever reduction, and the treatment of digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
